    • Reduced base damage from 24 to 22.
    • This includes Vex Mythoclast and SUROS Regime with Dual Speed Receiver but excludes Cerberus+1 and Sweet Business in its non-spun up state.
  • Adaptives
    • Increased base damage from 15 to 15.5 and critical hit damage from 26.25 to 27.1.

Pulse Rifles

  • Lightweights
    • Reduced base damage from 19.7 to 18.8 and critical hit damage from 31.5 to 30.1.
  • Legacy Frame
    • Since these weapons inherit the damage change from Lightweight Pulse Rifles, we are reverting the nerf to their ADS damage falloff scalar.
    • Increased ADS Damage Falloff Scalar from 1.6 back to 1.7.
  • Micro Missiles
    • Reduced Ammo Generation stat of legendary Micro Missile Pulse Rifles by 15.

Scout Rifles

  • Rapid Fires
    • Reduced base damage from 28 to 27.3 and critical hit damage from 54.6 to 53.25.

Hand Cannons

  • Aggressives
    • Reduced base damage from 49 to 48.7 and critical hit damage from 90.65 to 90.1.

Bows

  • General
    • Increased damage versus base combatants by an additional 20%.
    • Increased damage versus elite combatants by an additional 40%.
  • Lightweight
    • Fixed an issue where some Lightweight Bows (Whispering Slab reprise) were displaying an incorrect draw time.

Special Ammo Weapons

  • General
    • Increased damage versus elite combatants by 20%.
      • Excludes Snipers, Rocket Pulses, Rocket Sidearms, and Choir of One.

Rocket Pulses

  • Decreased PvE damage by 10%.
  • Decreased damage vs bosses by 25%.
    • This is on top of the above 10%.
  • Reduced projectile explosion radius by 25%.
  • Reduced Super Generation by 25% for roamers and 33% for standard Supers.

Rocket Sidearms

  • Increased projectile explosion radius by 33%.

Fusion Rifles

  • Rapid Fire
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 7%.
  • Adaptive
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 10%.
  • Precision
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 20%.
  • High Impacts
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 15%.

Shotguns

  • Aggressive
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 15%.
    • Improved the base stats of Felwinter’s
  • Lightweight
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 15%.
  • Precision
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 20%.

Slug Shotguns

  • Rapid Fire
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 10%.
  • Pinpoint
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 5%.
  • Heavy Burst
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 10%.

Snipers

  • Aggressive
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 15%.
  • Adaptive
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 10%.

Glaives

  • Rapid Fire
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 10%.

Trace Rifles

  • General
    • Increased damage versus base combatants by an additional 20%.
    • Increased damage versus elite combatants by an additional 20%.
  • Exotic Trace Rifles
    • Increased bonus damage versus base combatants from 30% to 40%.

Heavy Grenade Launchers

  • General
    • Increased damage versus Bosses and Champions by 5%.

Rocket Launchers

  • General
    • Increased damage versus Bosses and Champions by 5%.

Crossbows

  • General
    • Increased damage versus Bosses and Champions by 10%.

Linear Fusion Rifles

  • Precisions
    • Increased PvE Damage Scalar by 10%.
    • Increased ammo reserves by ~30%.
  • General
    • Increased the damage of legendary LFRs versus Bosses and Champions by 10%.

Machine Guns

  • Heat Weapons
    • Increased Bitter End’s reserve ammo.

Swords

  • Decreased cost of Vortex frame heavy attack from six to four ammo.

Exotic Weapons

Tarrabah

  • Dealing damage to Scorched combatants grants increased Ravenous Beast progress by 33%.
    • Can generate full meter if all the mag hits a scorched enemy.
  • Kills grant greatly increased progress.
    • 10% per kill.

Anarchy

  • Increased armed trap lifetime 50% from 10 to 15 seconds.
  • Increased trap damage versus combatants by 30%.
  • Doubled trap detonation damage vs combatants to better match the per tick damage of a trap.
  • Replaced Moving Target with Impromptu Ammunition.

Divinity

  • Increased Penance damage by 20%.
  • Reduced time to activate Penance from four to three seconds.
  • Reduced shots to activate the cage from 14 to 10.

Winterbite

  • Reduced self damage of the projectile by 66%.

Edge of Action, Concurrence, and Intent

  • Glaive projectiles now grant doubled progress towards special shot.

Crimson

  • Now has To the Pain as a catalyst perk in addition to the stat increase.

Coldheart

  • Now has Supercharged Magazine as a catalyst perk in addition to the stat increase.

Merciless

  • Impetus’s bonuses can now be refreshed while active.
  • Now has Heal Clip as a catalyst perk in addition to the stat increase.

Sunshot

  • Now has Loose Change as a catalyst perk in addition to the stat increase.

Skyburner’s Oath

  • Increased base handling by 20.
  • Now has Incandescent and Burning Ambition as catalyst perks in addition to the stat increase.

Riskrunner

  • Now has Chain Reaction as a catalyst perk in addition to the stat increase.

The Prospector

  • Now has Danger Zone as a catalyst perk in addition to the stat increase.

Hard Light

  • Now has [REDACTED] as a catalyst perk in addition to the stat increase.
  • Slightly tweaked the special reload activation and animation cancel timings.
    • This should feel more snappy and responsive.

Borealis

  • Now has Shield Disorient as a catalyst perk in addition to the stat increase.
  • Slightly tweaked the special reload activation and animation cancel timings.
    • This should feel more snappy and responsive.

Dead Messenger

  • Slightly tweaked the special reload activation and animation cancel timings.
    • This should feel more snappy and responsive.

Hierarchy of Needs

  • Seekers now apply +15 (+5) scorch to hit targets.
  • Added increased hip fire accuracy to the benefits provided by the catalyst.
  • Added hidden tracking for seekers that guides them to targets hit by precision damage.

Touch of Malice

  • Increased final round bonus damage by 10%.
  • Set it up so the Special Reload can be made faster by buffing reload speed.
  • Replaced Rapid Hit in the catalyst with Precision Instrument.
  • Gain increased stability and reload speed with each stack of Charged with Blight.

Jade Rabbit

  • Increased the magazine size from 10 to 15.
  • Removed Zen Moment. Added Shoot to Loot and Firefly as the two traits.
  • Fate of All Fools
    • Precision kills will now add a stack.
    • Gaining a stack now refills the magazine.
    • Increased the maximum stacks cap to 15.
    • Stacks will no longer be completely removed when you reload. Dealing direct body shot damage will remove one stack.
    • Scales the damage of Firefly when it is active, and dealing splash damage will not remove a stack of the perk.
  • Catalyst
    • In addition to adding Stability, special reloading will consume Fate of All Fools and grants a singular Micro Missile round which deals bonus damage based on the number of stacks.

Vex Mythoclast

  • Timeless Mythoclast (Intrinsic)
    • Now has what is comparably a more potent version of Rewind Rounds while in full auto mode.
  • Temporal Unlimiter (Trait)
    • Precision kills with Linear Fusion Rifle shots now trigger an ignition.
    • After leaving Linear Fusion Rifle mode, the magazine is now fully reloaded.
    • The HUD buff and magazine display have been tweaked to show the exact amount of Linear Fusion shots remaining. This is a purely visual change; functionality remains the same.
  • Calculated Balance (Catalyst)
    • Sustained damage can now also activate the catalyst in addition to kills.
    • Added a HUD buff.

The Last Word

  • Increased stability on mouse and keyboard by 10%.
  • Increased aim assist when hip firing on mouse and keyboard by 10%.

Trinity Ghoul

  • Fixed an issue where kills with Lightning Rod chains were not granting ammo progress.

MIDA Multi-Tool

  • Decreased the PvE combatant kill requirement from ten kills per modification (15 with Macro-Tool) to seven kills per modification (ten with Macro-Tool). This lowers the total kill requirement from 40 (60) to 27 (40) to acquire all modifications.
  • The catalyst’s improved radar resolution now remains active for a few seconds when you stow Multi-Tool instead of immediately deactivating. Multi-Tool still needs to be in your hands for the catalyst to activate.

Sunshot

  • Rebalanced the damage of the explosion to deal more damage up close and less damage further away.

Graviton Lance

  • Rebalanced the damage of the explosion to deal more damage up close and less damage further away.

Outbreak Perfected

  • Reduced damage bonus at maximum Nanite stacks from 4.5x to 4x.

Arbalest

  • Increased damage versus Champions by 33%.

Choir of One

  • Reduced ammo per brick.
    • Base: 45 to 35.
    • Enhanced: 90 to 70.

Wardcliff Coil

  • Fixed an issue where firing Wardcliff Coil would not grant Bolt Charge if the firing player was too far off the ground.
  • Mechanized Autoloader now works while the weapon is stowed.
  • Increased maximum ammo from five to eight.

Sleeper Simulant

  • Increased damage versus Bosses and Champions by 10%.

The Lament

  • Reverted the previous nerf to this weapon by ~70%.
    • Returned most of the final combo damage and self healing the previous change reduced.

Truth

  • Now returns one ammo to reserves and buffs the next magazine when all rounds in a magazine hit a target.
    • The next magazine gains dramatically increased rate of fire, stability, tracking strength, and cluster bombs.

Thunderlord

  • Decreased ammo refunded by the lightning strike from seven to five at base.

Whirling Ovation

  • Increased the amount of Anticipation gained per point of damage by just over 11%.
    • This is compensation for the Rocket Pulse damage nerf disproportionately affecting the feel of this weapon’s rotations.

Heirloom

  • Increased charge rate by 20%.
  • Increased impact damage of charged shots by 20% in PvE only.
  • Reduced extra heat generated by charged shots by 7%.
  • Increased damage vs miniboss and champion targets by 20%.
  • Increased damage vs boss targets by 10%.
  • Added Incandescent perk to catalyst.

Prometheus Lens

  • Increased scorch stacks applied per trigger.
    • Up to 20 (+10) from 10 (+10).

Centrifuse

  • Increased the amount of magazine refilled per trigger to 10% from 4%.

Slayer’s Fang

  • Increased magazine size by two.
  • Increased reserve ammo by ten.
  • Now slowly reloads itself while its buff is active.

Xenophage

  • Increased reserve ammo by ten.

Chaperone

  • Increased magazine size by one.

Ice Breaker

  • Fixed an issue where Ice Breaker was errantly igniting Barrier Champions while they were stunned.

Praxic Blade

  • Decreased the Balanced Form’s ammo generation from two to one.
  • Changed the Defensive Form’s ammo criteria to rapid blocks.
  • Decreased the tracking of the reflected bolts at long range targets.
  • Can now reflect projectiles from Fallen turrets.

Tractor Cannon

  • Adjusted Tractor Cannon’s perk description to more clearly indicate that it applies the Void suppression effect and a more powerful Void weaken rather than a unique debuff.
    • Also adjusted the language used on both Hunter supers that apply a non-standard Weaken effect (Deadfall and Mobius Quiver), as well as Felwinter’s Helm.
    • The functionality of these effects has not changed.

Weapon perks

Collective Action, Collective Pugilism, Cooling Baubles, Elemental Charge, Facet of Honor

  • These perks now all share the same Tangle activator.
  • Previously this activator differed depending on the perk, and would either activate when shooting a Tangle, picking up a Tangle, throwing a Tangle, or a combination of these despite sharing the same terminology in their strings.
  • These now share the same activator: destroy a Tangle, either by shooing it or upon impact after throwing it.
  • In addition to this activator, Collective Action and Collective Pugilism also grant additional time to their durations upon throwing a Tangle. This unique behavior is called out in their descriptions.
  • Artifact elemental orbs are now officially considered elemental pickups for the sake of these perks and grant the same benefits as Tangles. As such, we fixed an issue where the artifact Arc orbs would give dark Transcendence energy with Facet of Honor; they now give light Transcendence energy.

All-Star

  • Damage timer is paused on weapon stow and resumed on weapon equip.

Advanced Reflexes (Dreaming City Origin Trait)

  • New effect: +5% damage bonus while active.
  • Increased bonus handling from +30 to +40.
  • Increased bonus charge rate (Swords) from +20 to +35.

Gun and Run (Gambit Origin Trait)

  • Reworked Effect: Defeating targets builds stacks of Gun and Run for a short duration. Sprinting for a short time consumes a stack to partially reload this weapon’s magazine from reserves; on Bows, this briefly improves draw speed.

Nanotech Tracers Missiles (Neomuna Destination Origin Trait)

  • Decreased the amount of hits needed for Basso Ostinato to activate Nanotech Tracers Missiles by roughly one.

Jolting Feedback

  • Decreased shots to activate the perk by ~1-2 for Scout Rifles and Hand Cannons.

Focused Fury

  • Increased damage bonus from 20% to 25%.
  • Decreased hits required to activate from 50% of the magazine to 35% of the magazine.

Full Court

  • Increased maximum explosive damage bonus from 25% to 35%.

Lasting Impression

  • Increased explosive damage bonus from 25% to 35%.

Reservoir Burst

  • Increased damage bonus from 25% (27.5% enhanced) to 30% (33% enhanced).

Encore

  • Encore’s HUD buff now displays a timer.

Adagio

  • Decreased the damage bonus on Hand Cannons from 30% to 27%.
  • Increased the damage bonus on Shotguns and Bows from 20% to 30%.

Collective Action

  • Instead of a flat seven seconds, elemental pickup now provides differing amounts of time to the perk duration depending on how easy they are to create. These values match Collective Pugilism:
    • Firesprites, Void Breaches: eight seconds.
    • Ionic Traces, Stasis Shards: four seconds.
    • Tangle Detonation: four seconds.
    • Tangle Throw: four seconds.
  • To compensate for the lower duration gained from some elemental pickups, the damage bonus against PVE combatants has been increased from 20% to 30%. The damage bonus against players remains at 10%.
  • The enhanced benefit no longer provides more time per pickup, but instead increases the damage bonus to 33% against PVE combatants, and 11% against players.
  • Increased maximum time from 12 to 16 seconds.

Full Choke

  • Decreased pellet spread by 5% instead of 3.25%.

Shot Package intrinsic

  • Decreased pellet spread by 2.5% in ADS (aim down sight).

Frenzy

  • Fixed an issue where the enhanced version of Frenzy was not getting an extended duration when refreshed compared to standard Frenzy.
  • Fixed an issue where Frenzy being inactive could prevent the Crucible origin perk from activating.

Gutshot Straight

  • Decreased the aim assist penalty by 20% on the base perk and 25% on the enhanced perk.
  • Fixed an issue where this perk did not work on Bitter End.

Envious Assassin

  • Fixed an issue where the perk was not working on some single shot weapons.

Cascade Point

  • Since we removed the “Ready” HUD buff when the weapon is stowed, we have simplified the activation criteria for dealing precision damage with other weapons:
    • Auto Rifles, Pulse Rifles, SMGs, Trace Rifles, Sidearms, LMGs: six critical hits.
    • Hand Cannons, Scout Rifles: four critical hits.
    • Bows, LFRs, Glaive projectiles, Shotguns, Sniper Rifles: two critical hits.

Attrition Orbs

  • Now activates in four hits on wave frame grenade launchers, down from ten.
  • Now activates in three hits on rocket launchers, down from six.

Contending Cascade

  • The number of rapid final blows needed get max benefits has been reduced from 15 (12 if enhanced) to 12 (10 if enhanced).
  • Stat benefits are now maxed out when the elemental explosions are granted instead of scaling with up to 20 rapid final blows.

Desperado

  • In addition to its current activator of reloading after a precision kill, Desperado can now also activate when reloading after three rapid kills.

Physic

  • Can now roll on damage types other than Solar.

Miscellaneous

  • Deprecated Renegades fragile weapon mods.
  • Increased stats for several reprised weapons. Changes listed here apply to both old and new versions of these weapons.
    • Note that weapons that have changed archetype in their reprised form are not listed here; their old and new versions have separate stat packages.
    • Battle Scar
      • +5 Range
      • +8 Stability
      • +1 Recoil Direction
    • Seventh Seraph Carbine
      • +5 Range
      • +5 Aim Assistance
    • Seventh Seraph Officer Revolver
      • +6 Range
      • +6 Aim Assistance
    • Seventh Seraph SI-2
      • +10 Range
      • +10 Airborne Effectiveness
      • +5 Aim Assistance
    • Seventh Seraph VY-7
      • +3 Range
    • Seventh Seraph SAW
      • +5 Magazine
      • +5 Handling
      • +30 Recoil Direction
    • True Prophecy
      • +2 Recoil Direction
      • +10 Airborne Effectiveness
    • The Deicide
      • +5 Stability
      • +2 Aim Assistance
    • Pluperfect
      • +13 Stability
      • +5 Airborne Effectiveness
      • +2 Aim Assistance
    • Cry Mutiny
      • +2 Magazine
    • Spare Rations
      • +10 Range
      • +2 Stability
    • Loud Lullaby
      • +5 Range
      • +10 Stability
      • +4 Handling
      • +5 Aim Assist
    • Premonition
      • +8 Stability
      • +7 Reload Speed
      • +7 Handling
      • +5 Aim Assist
    • Every Waking Moment
      • +5 Range
      • +7 Reload Speed
      • +6 Handling
    • Added a filtering option for the Vault based on a weapon’s anti-Champion capabilities.
    • Following the HUD buff iteration we did for subclass buffs in v950, we wanted to iterate on weapon HUD buffs for v970. Our goal is to improve the player experience by improving clarity concerning the source of a buff (ie. if you see a buff on screen it’s from your active weapon), minimizing the amount of buffs that get pushed out by removing unnecessary buffs, and when buffs do get pushed out they are of lesser importance. Key notes include:
      • A handful of weapon traits’ buff priority have been adjusted so that more important buffs should be less likely to get pushed out.
      • Perk HUD buffs that appeared while stowed will now require that weapon to be your active weapon. Functionality remains the same for perks that have effects that remain active while stowed.
      • Icarus Grip and Sprint Grip no longer have HUD buffs.
      • Gordian Knot Cooldown now appears in the weapon buff channel.
    • Weapon of Sorrow damage-over-time
      • Similar to the work done for micro-missiles, we have updated the term for the damage over time debuff applied by Weapons of Sorrow in order to more clearly define this as a unique debuff and to link these weapons and armor to each other. This will be reflected in their perk descriptions, and does not affect functionality.
      • “Corruption” now refers to the short-duration damage-over-time that can stack.
        • Thorn, Touch of Malice, and Necrochasm all apply this.
      • “Potent corruption” now refers to the longer duration damage-over-time that cannot stack and will (typically) gradually increase in damage.
        • Necrotic Grip, Spirit of the Necrotic, and Osteo Striga all apply this. Spirit of the Necrotic will continue to persist at the same damage value (not ramp up).

Weapon fixes

  • Fixed an issue where Meganeura’s detonation was scaling damage incorrectly across archetypes, causing it to deal less damage than intended on most weapons.
  • Fixed an issue where Sullen Claw’s swing speed stat displayed the incorrect value. This is a purely visual fix.
  • Fixed an issue where the application of scorch and ignition damage could trigger Sharp Harvest.
  • Fixed an issue where Basso Ostinato could trigger Nanotech Tracer Missiles without damaging an enemy.
  • Fixed Hunter tether super not activating Aggregate Charge’s damage buff unless another debuff is on the target.
  • Fixed an issue with Hard Light, Borealis, and Dead Messenger where The Fundamentals could only be activated once per life.
  • Paracausal Fluid
    • Fixed an issue where the HUD buff could display stacks at x2, despite multiple stacks providing no additional benefit.
    • Fixed an issue where this perk would not activate on Swords.
    • Fixed an issue where this perk would not always activate if the enemy had a high enough health pool.
  • Fixed an issue where controller rumble was not playing correctly when venting heat weapons.
  • Fixed an issue where the Charge Time mod on Fusion Rifles and Linear Fusion Rifles was not decreasing charge time.
  • Fixed an issue where Necrotic Grip wasn’t granting Necrochasm a bonus to airborne effectiveness.
  • Fixed an issue where corruption from Thorn, Necrochasm, or Touch of Malice could extend the duration of potent corruption from Osteo Striga or Necrotic Grip. Likewise, fixed an issue where potent corruption from Osteo Striga or Necrotic Grip could remove all stacks of corruption applied by Thorn, Necrochasm, or Touch of Malice.
    • Both types of corruption can now be active on the same enemy at the same time.
  • Fixed an issue where freezing an enemy would remove Weapon of Sorrow corruption from that enemy.
  • Fixed an issue where kills with Crossbow Charged Bolts would trigger the Osteo Striga catalyst.
  • Fixed the reload and special reload timings on various weapons to be slightly sooner so that they align with the animation:
    • Delicate Tomb basic reload.
    • Edge of Action, Intent, and Concurrence special reload.
    • Wolfsbane special reload.
    • Glaive basic reload.
  • Fixed perk feedback and HUD buffs on Edge of Action, Intent, and Concurrence to feel slightly more responsive and easier to read.
  • Fixed an issue where the owner or ally of an Edge of Action’s bubble could deal damage to and destroy the bubble.
  • Fixed an issue where Monte Carlo bayonet kills would not grant ammo meter progress.
  • Fixed an issue where Monte Carlo could get into a state where the catalyst perk was active but the bayonet was not active.
  • Fixed an issue where Monte Carlo’s bayonet melee was dealing more damage against ultras than intended while playing as a Titan.
  • Fixed the timing of the firing and reload controller rumble on Glaives to more accurately align with their actions.
  • Fixed an issue where Withering Gaze on Cusp Sempiternal wouldn’t weaken unless you were aiming when the projectile made contact.
  • Fixed an issue where Two-Tailed Fox would not benefit from the Arc reloader mod after its catalyst was inserted.
  • Fixed an issue where Jötunn displayed zero magazine size in the inspection screen.
  • These weapons’ projectiles are now capable of over-penetrating combatant shields while they have Anti-Barrier capabilities:
    • Rocket launchers
    • Hierarchy of Needs submunitions
    • Caster Frame Swords
  • Fixed an issue where Dragon’s Breath rockets weren’t detaching properly from enemies or objects that died or despawned.
  • Fixed an issue where Merciless couldn’t gain stacks of Conserve Momentum when shooting through a Banner Shield.
  • Fixed an issue where picking up a Kinetic brick would convert the damage type of some non-Kinetic weapons to Kinetic, and give ammo to some non-Kinetic weapons.
  • Fixed an issue where Machine Gun projectiles were unable to apply the ‘increased damage’ impact VFX from various traits.
  • Fixed an issue where enhanced Omolon Fluid Dynamics didn’t display the tooltip for the enhanced benefit.
  • New Land Beyond: Fixed an issue with Bullseye Bolster where precision hits wouldn’t refresh the perk’s timer while the benefits were already active.
  • Fixed an issue where Adrenaline Junkie would not activate when triggered by non-standard grenade abilities like Hellion or grapple melee.
  • Fixed an issue where The Bombardiers were applying custom blind and suppress effects, instead of Arc Blind and Void Suppress
    • In fixing this, the duration of the blind effect against PvE combatants was increased from five seconds to ten seconds, matching the duration of suppression.

Abilities

General

Grenades

  • NEW: Stasis Shatter Grenade
    • A grenade that sticks to combatants before exploding into razor-sharp Stasis shrapnel. The shrapnel ricochets off surfaces, dealing damage to all in its path. Hitting a frozen target shatters it instantly and deals bonus damage.
  • NEW: Strand Slicewire Grenade
    • A grenade that bounces and releases multiple whirling threads, severing targets.
  • Spike Grenade
    • Slightly increased the damage area at the wider side of the cone.
  • Lightning Grenade
    • Increased detonation damage by 25%.
  • Scatter Grenades
    • Reduced chase distance versus players by 50%.
    • Reduce the amount of Grenade energy this ability gains from perks by 12.5%.
  • Grapple
    • Against boss combatants, decreased max grapple melee damage bonus granted by grenade stat by 50%.
    • Against boss combatants, damage bonus granted by Grenade stat and Melee stat is now additive instead of multiplicative.

Melees

  • When powered melee and unpowered melee are bound to the same button, pressing that button now behaves consistently when aiming down sights (ADS).
    • Previously: ADS and pressing melee triggers unpowered melee.
    • Now: ADS and pressing melee triggers powered melee if it is charged, unpowered melee otherwise.

Supers

  • Reduced Super energy generation from dealing damage to bosses by 60%.

Multi-charge Abilities

  • In PvP only, when the match begins, multi-charge abilities now start with one charge instead of all charges.

Ability Cooldown Stats

  • Reduced maximum passive ability-cooldown-bonus granted by ability stats by ~20%.
    • Curve rescaled to reach max at the same ratio.
  • Reduced maximum active ability-cooldown-bonus granted by ability stats from 190% to 125%.
    • Curve rescaled to reach max at the same ratio.

Stasis

  • Shatter
    • Stasis shatter damage now stuns Unstoppable Champions instead of Overload Champions.
      • Note: This is a revert of a previous change.
    • Frost Armor (Status Effect)
      • Frost Armor stacks now melt off one at a time instead of all at once.
      • Each stack now gives slightly more damage resistance.
        • Damage resistance at five stacks is increased from ~31% to ~35%.
      • Whisper of Rime (Fragment)
        • Decreased max stacks from eight to seven.
        • Max damage resistance is still 50%.

Arc

  • Spark of Shock (Status Effect)
    • When jolt is applied via an Arc Soul or an Ionic Sentry, there is now a four-second internal cooldown for applying jolt again via these constructs.

Void

  • Invisibility (Status Effect)
    • Made Void Invisibility slightly more visible in Crucible.

Prismatic

  • Facet of Dominance (Fragment)
    • When jolt is applied via an Arc Soul, there is now a four-second internal cooldown for applying jolt again via this construct.

Strand

  • Grapple Tangles
    • Fixed an issue where players who didn’t have the Grapple Grenade equipped could Grapple Punch from a free grapple.

Titan

Sunbreaker

  • NEW: Shieldburst (Aspect)
    • Enhances your equipped barricade with Solar capabilities.
      • Towering Barricade: Your barricade slides forward across the ground before stopping, allowing you to advance in safety.
      • Rally Barricade: Standing behind your barricade grants scorching rounds to your allies’ Solar and Kinetic weapons.
    • While your barricade is deployed, activate your class ability again to remotely detonate it, scorching combatants caught in the detonation. Dealing damage increases the power of the blast.
  • Hammer Strike (Melee)
    • Increased damage versus combatants by 100%.

Sentinel

  • Ward of Dawn (Super)
    • Ability rework: Ward of Dawn now creates a much larger dome that you can fight inside.
    • Ward of Dawn now has a core object that destroys the ward if it’s destroyed.
      • The casting Titan can pick up the core object and throw it to relocate the Ward up to two times.
    • Allies inside the dome now gain Weapons of Light.
    • The dome’s owner now gains increased melee damage.
    • Enemies inside the dome are now weakened.
    • On deployment, now pulls nearby enemies into the dome.
    • Minor combatants inside the dome are now taunted by the players inside.
    • The Ward now persists after the casting Titan dies.
  • Offensive Bulwark (Aspect)
    • While the player has a Void Overshield, final blows now grant melee ability energy.
      • The grenade energy generation has also been rolled into this and is now granted on final blow instead of as increased recharge rate.
    • Unbreakable (Aspect)
      • Final blows with Unbreakable’s blast now grant Devour.
      • Slightly increased the size of Unbreakable’s blast.
    • Bastion (Aspect)
      • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
    • Shield Bash (Melee)
      • Increased damage vsersus combatants by 100%.

Striker

  • Thruster (Class Ability)
    • Can now be activated while airborne.
  • Knockout (Aspect)
    • No longer grants a lunge-range increase vs. enemy players.
  • Touch of Thunder (Aspect)
    • Lightning Grenade
      • Now grants five stacks of Bolt Charge per hit.
    • Flashbang Grenade
      • The blinding pulse on first bounce now deals damage.
    • Storm Grenade
      • Increased storm duration by 0.5 seconds.
      • Increased maximum tracking speed versus combatants by 38%.
      • Increased time to reach max tracking speed by 25%.
    • Seismic Strike (Melee)
      • Increased damage versus combatants by 100%.
    • Knockout
      • Corrected an issue where the bonus damage could be applied too early, increasing the damage of the melee that activated it and causing unexpected one hit kills in Crucible.

 Behemoth

  • Tectonic Harvest (Aspect)
    • All Stasis subclasses now create Shatter Shards intrinsically.
    • Glacial Harvest gives Frost Armor when you pick up a Shatter Shard.
    • While you have Frost Armor, defeating an enemy with a Stasis or Kinetic weapon has a chance to shatter the enemy, dealing damage around the shattered enemy.
      • This chance increases based on how many Frost Armor stacks you have.
    • While you have Frost Armor, taking damage now grants grenade energy.
    • Decreased Fragment slots from three to two.
  • Diamond Lance (Aspect)
    • Decreased size of freeze detonation from thrown Diamond Lance versus players.
  • Cryoclasm (Aspect)
    • Sliding while you have Frost Armor now launches a Stasis wave that shatters Stasis Crystals and frozen enemies in front of you.
  • Glacial Quake (Super)
    • Heavy attacks now deal additional damage in an area in front of the Titan.

Berserker

  • Drengr’s Lash (Aspect)
    • Defeating elementally debuffed targets now grants Class Ability energy.
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
    • The suspending wave’s impact damage now scales with your Class stat when your Class stat is above 100.

Hunter

Nightstalker

  • NEW: Phantom Surge (Melee)
    • Dash forward, slashing combatants as you pass through them. Deals significantly increased damage to Void-debuffed targets and targets marked by On The Prowl.
    • Generate Void Overshield for each combatant hit. Defeating at least one combatant grants additional Overshield and refunds some melee ability energy.
  • Trapper’s Ambush (Aspect)
    • Now deals significant area damage on impact instead of creating a smoke cloud.
      • Damage and radius is increased if cast while you have a Void buff.
    • Now uses class-ability energy instead of melee energy, and counts as a class ability for all perks and abilities.
    • If cast while you also have Vanishing Step equipped, you create a smoke cloud on landing (previous behavior).
    • Damage now scales with your Class stat when your Class stat is above 100.
  • On the Prowl (Aspect)
    • In addition to acquiring a target when you become invisible, can now also passively acquire a target after a short time if you’re not invisible.
  • Smoke Bomb (Melee)
    • Now natively grants invisibility to yourself and allies on deployment.
  • Mobius Quiver (Super)
    • Reduced maximum Moebius Quiver buff to Crossbow weapons by 50%.

Gunslinger

  • NEW: Crackshot (Aspect)
    • Activate your class ability to fire up to three scorching shots at targets you’ve marked. Aim at targets to mark them.
    • Landing all three shots cures you.
  • Acrobat’s Dodge (Class Ability)
    • Now can be activated while airborne.
  • Proximity Explosive Knife (Melee)
    • Increased detonation size from 5 to 6 meters.
    • Increased detonation damage from 90 to 100.
  • On Your Mark (Aspect)
    • Now grants Solar Restoration x1 for 3 seconds after final blows at ten stacks.
    • Final blows with Red Death Reformed grant Cure 1 + Resto 1 for 3 sec.
  • Knock ’em Down (Aspect)
    • Now grants Cure on super activation.
  • Gunpowder Gamble (Aspect)
    • Fixed an issue where the Gunpowder Gamble projectile could remain in the Hunter’s hand after throwing it.
  • Blade Barrage (Super)
    • Increased knife projectile speed and tracking.
    • Increased knife detonation size from 3 to 4.25 meters.
    • Increased knife detonation damage by 10%.
    • Knife detonations now Scorch enemies.
  • Golden Gun: Deadshot (Super)
    • Reduced super-energy gain from player kills by 40%.

Arcstrider

  • Arc Staff (Super)
    • Now grants Bolt Charge stacks on kill.
  • Lethal Current (Aspect)
    • Now jolts and creates an aftershock when used with Tempest Strike.
    • Now can be activated while in Super.
    • Fixed an issue where Lethal Current would sometimes deactivate before providing the aftershock benefits.
  • Ascension (Aspect)
    • Ascension detonation damage now scales with your Class stat when your Class stat is above 100.
    • Now counts as a class ability for all perks and abilities.

Threadrunner

  • Rope Dart (Melee)
    • Successfully catching the rope dart now grants Woven Mail based on the number of targets it defeated.
  • Widow’s Silk (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
    • Fixed a bug where Widow’s Silk was not granting an extra grapple charge when using Silkstrike.
  • Threaded Specter (Aspect)
    • The specter’s detonation damage now scales with your Class stat when your Class stat is above 100.
  • Ensnaring Slam
    • Now counts as a class ability for all perks and abilities.

Revenant

  • Grim Harvest (Aspect)
    • All Stasis subclasses now create Shatter Shards intrinsically.
    • Glacial Harvest gives Frost Armor when you pick up a Shatter Shard.
    • While you have Frost Armor, defeating an enemy with a Stasis or Kinetic weapon has a chance to shatter the enemy, dealing damage around the shattered enemy.
      • This chance increases based on how many Frost Armor stacks you have.
    • While you have Frost Armor, taking damage now grants class-ability energy.
    • Decreased Fragment slots from three to two.
  • Shatterdive (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
  • Winter’s Shroud (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.

Prismatic

  • Ascension (Aspect)
    • Can now activate the Winter’s Shroud Aspect on cast.

Warlock

Voidwalker

  • NEW: Soul Siphon (Aspect)
    • Ready this aspect by striking a foe with your powered melee ability.
    • Once readied, press your melee ability to begin siphoning energy from all enemies in front of you, dealing damage and gathering Overshield and class ability energy from each target.
  • Child of the Old Gods (Aspect)
    • Now suppresses combatants for a short time after initial attach.
    • Increased the Child’s max speed and acceleration by 100%.
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
    • Fixed an issue where defeating enemies who were being drained by the Child with weapons would not provide class-ability energy.
  • Nova Bomb: Cataclysm (Super)
    • Allied projectiles now pass through the Nova Bomb projectile instead of colliding with it.

Dawnblade

  • Heat Rises (Aspect)
    • While Heat Rises is active, now reduces the accuracy of combatant attacks versus the Warlock.
  • Hellion (Aspect)
    • Reduced maximum target acquisition range from 40 to 32 meters.
  • Icarus Dash (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.

Stormcaller

  • Chain Lightning (Melee)
    • Increased damage vs. combatants by 100%.
    • Now has two ability charges by default.
  • Arc Soul (Aspect)
    • Reduced maximum target acquisition range from 30 to 24 meters.
  • Lightning Surge (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.

Shadebinder

  • Glacial Harvest (Aspect)
    • All Stasis subclasses now create Shatter Shards intrinsically.
    • Glacial Harvest gives Frost Armor when you pick up a Shatter Shard.
    • While you have Frost Armor, defeating an enemy with a Stasis or Kinetic weapon has a chance to shatter the enemy, dealing damage around the shattered enemy.
    • While you have Frost Armor, taking damage now grants grenade energy.
    • Decreased Fragment slots from three to two.
  • Frostpulse (Aspect)
    • Now grants five stacks of Frost Armor if this ability freezes a target.
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
  • Winter’s Wrath (Super)
    • Winter’s Wrath projectiles now prioritize targets in alignment with their trajectory over closer targets.

Broodweaver

  • Mindspun Invocation (Aspect)
    • Threadling Grenade no longer creates perched Threadlings when consumed. Instead, Threadling Grenade can now be charged and thrown, spawning a Threadling nest (similar to Service of Luzaku) on impact.
  • The Wanderer (Aspect)
    • Threadling final blows now reduce your Tangle cooldown by one second.
  • Weavewalk (Aspect)
    • Melee attacks can no longer interrupt the Weavewalk exit animation.
  • Needlestorm (Super)
    • Fixed an issue where Needlestorm projectiles would not track certain bosses.

Healing Rift and Empowering Rift

  • Fixed an issue where Warlock Rifts could spawn under the ground if the player was throttling towards certain environmental objects.

Artifacts 2.0

  • Introducing Artifacts 2.0:
    • Simplified and streamlined how artifact perks are chosen.
    • Brought back a selection of Artifacts from previous Seasons, all of which have been updated to the Artifacts 2.0 system.
      • Players will have access to a total of seven Artifacts.
    • Removed Anti-Champion mods from all available Artifacts, as this functionality has been moved to being intrinsic to all weapons.
      • See our Anti-Champion 2.0 section.
    • Artifact mods are now disabled in competitive Crucible modes. This includes both the Glory playlist and Trials matches.
    • Removed New Gear bonus. Previous damage and DR increases have been folded into the base behavior for weapons and armor.
    • Fixed an issue where Seasonal Artifacts in Collections with visual stages did not display as if they had been fully unlocked.
    • Fixed an issue where some Seasonal Artifacts in Collections appeared as your equipped Ghost.

Queensfoil Censer

  • Torch
    • No longer increases damage versus bosses.
  • Heart of the Flame
    • Reduced top end to 15% increased damage for Supers based on nearby allies from 21%.
  • Bring into Being
    • Set equal to wearing three pieces of old season armor, perk can activate if the user has 60% Super energy or more.
  • Dragons Bite
    • Set to the value of wearing three pieces of armor, every three kills.
  • Creeping Chill
    • Now works with any Stasis defeat instead of just weapons. Also grants Frost Armor to allies near the explosion radius.
  • Solo Operative
    • Updated to be “precision final blows grant a scaling damage benefit with weapons until the user dies.”

Slayer Baron Apothecary Satchel

  • Wind Chill
    • Precision damage is no longer required to activate this perk.
  • Debilitating Wave
    • Significantly increased the tracking capabilities of the wave.
  • Curative Orbs
    • This perk no longer activates when picking up a Tangle but instead when destroying a Tangle (either by shooting it or upon impact after throwing it).
  • Void Renewal
    • The benefits from the old artifact perk Frenzied Hunger are now also granted upon collecting a Void Breach. Slightly increased these benefits to match the new activation requirement.
  • Kinetic Impacts
    • Special and Primary ammo Grenade Launchers can now also trigger this perk. The shockwave from Special and Primary ammo Grenade Launchers deals less damage than from Heavy Grenade Launchers.
    • The benefits from the old artifact perk ‘Rapid Impacts’ has been rolled into this perk.
  • Old God’s Rite
    • Increased the base damage of the projectiles by roughly 200%. In addition to this, the projectiles deal 85% more damage to orange bars, and 200% more damage to red bars.
  • Concussive Reload
    • This has been replaced with Weakened Clear. Armor of Eramis has been replaced with Frost Renewal.
  • Finders Keepers
    • This has been replaced with Killing Breeze.
  • Killing Breeze
    • Increased the duration from five to seven seconds.
  • The boost and/or seasonal gear bonus on these perks has been rolled into the base perk.
    • Wind Chill
    • Crystalline Converter
    • Total Carnage
    • Power From Pain
    • Trace Evidence
    • The Thick of It
    • Hail the Storm
    • Debilitating Wave
    • Retinal Burn
    • Curative Orbs
    • Void Renewal
    • Old God’s Rite
  • The boost and/or seasonal gear bonus on these perks has been removed.
    • Killing Breeze
    • Wind Chill
    • Brain Freeze
    • Conductive Cosmic Crystal
  • Fixed an issue where Crystalline Converter’s HUD buff would appear while at zero perk progress.

Hunters Journal

  • Sword Stamina
    • Decreased ammo granted to special swords on three rapid kills from three ammo to one ammo.
  • Sustained Fire
    • Decreased from Resist x3 to Resist x2.
  • Sustained Fire
    • Now works with Support Auto Rifles.
  • Shieldcrush
    • Updated to work with the Melee and Grenade stats.
  • Transference
    • Decreased number of kills needed for the max payout from 20 to 12.

Tablet of Ruin

  • Volatile Marksman
    • Boost portion ICD has been removed.
  • Elemental Siphon
    • Increased Super energy for picking up a matching pickup from 3% to 5%.
  • Vile Weave
    • Increased how many seconds are removed from Tangle cooldown from one to four seconds.
  • Flashover
    • Damage bonus reduced from 150% to 50%.
  • No Bell
    • Updated to be additive melee stacking at 85% (former high end), increased duration by 0.5 seconds.
  • Heavy Ordinance Regeneration
    • Increased benefits timer from nine to ten seconds.
  • Limit Break
    • Decreased benefit from 30% to 15%.
  • Gold from Lead
    • Decreased proc rate by ~50%.
  • Particle Reconstruction
    • Increased the minimum shots to proc by ~70%.

Implement of Curiosity

  • Pack Tactics
    • Increased maximum duration from seven to ten seconds.
  • Elemental Daze
    • Decreased damage of the blast by 25%.
  • Elemental Coalescence
    • Increased chance of creating the elemental pickup by 20%.
  • Energy Acceleration
    • Updated string to reference behavior vs Unstoppable Champions.
  • Solar Shrapnel
    • Updated spawning behavior to match Horde Shuttle. This will increase its potential in roaming content and reduce it versus bosses.
  • Elemental Overdrive
    • Increased boost from 10% to 22% to match 3x surge.
  • Iron Lord’s Vigor
    • Decreased ammo granted to special swords on three rapid kills from three ammo to one ammo.

Encrypted Data Disc

  • Kinetic Ammo Synthesis
    • Decreased ammo generation for Heavy weapons and Praxic Blade by 50%.
  • Payday
    • Decreased Super energy generation by 5%.
  • Snipers Meditation
    • Decreased the damage bonus by 5%.

NPA Repulsion Regulator

  • Improved Unraveling
    • Increased PvE damage bonus from 10% to 15%.
  • Bricks from Beyond
    • Now grants Heavy ammo bar progress instead of bricks.
  • Supernova
    • PvE weaken duration increased from six to eight seconds.
  • Lightning Strikes Twice
    • Now works with the Grenade stat.

Power and Progression

  • Power caps will not be increasing and Power level will not be reset.
    • The hard cap remains 550 Power and all your items will maintain their current Power levels.
  • Players will continue to find new rewards at minimum 300 Power, maintaining a change made in Renegades.
  • Portal Activity Reward Power
    • The Power of Portal Activity rewards are no longer capped per the difficulty level selected.
      • Earning an “A” grade (for example) will drop rewards at the same Power level, regardless of which difficulty you played.
    • The Power of Portal Activity rewards has been increased, allowing for faster progression.
      • “A” grades or higher will now continue to award positive Power progression all the way to the hard cap of 550.
      • Higher grades will continue to provide larger Power bonuses.
    • Miscellaneous rewards from sources throughout the game (such as pre-The Edge of Fate content) have been buffed.
      • These rewards will no longer be capped at 300 Power.
      • These rewards now have a chance to drop +1 Power up to 400 Power.
      • After 400 Power, these rewards will drop close to, but below, your current Power level.
    • Rewards purchased from legacy vendors have been buffed.
      • These rewards will no longer be capped at 300 Power.
      • These rewards will drop close to, but below, your current Power level.
    • Rewards pulled from Collections have been buffed to drop at 300 Power (up from 200).

Currencies and Materials

  • Updated max stack size limits on the following currencies:
    • Strange Coins
      • New Limit: 777
    • Enhancement Cores
      • New Limit: 9999
    • Enhancement Prisms
      • New Limit: 200
    • Ascendant Shard
      • New Limit: 50
    • Ascendant Alloy
      • New Limit: 50
    • Raid Banner
      • New Limit: 999
    • Fixed incorrectly assigned Collections reacquisition costs for several Ghost Shells, Ships, and Sparrows released since Episode: Revenant.

Experience

  • The weekly rest bonus remains available for the first 250K XP earned each week.
  • The weekly catch-up bonus introduced for the Lawless Rewards Pass has been removed. This bonus had further increased bonus XP each week to help keep Pass progression on track ahead of a Pass’ expiration date. Since the Lawless Rewards Pass has ended and the Triumphant Rewards Pass will not expire, this bonus has been retired.

Bright Dust

  • Increased Bright Dust gains across almost all weekly sources. On an annual basis, these increased earn rates will offset the loss of Bright Dust from new Rewards Passes and Seasonal Events.
    • Daily Objectives: 20 each (up from 10)
    • Weekly Objectives: 100 each (up from 50)
    • Weekly Hub Bonus: 20 (up from 10)
    • Weekly Reward Track: 1000 total (up from 650)
    • Active Orders (before Weekly Softcap): 1500 total (up from 1000)
      • 10 per Normal, 100 per Legendary, 200 per Exotic. These rates are unchanged.
    • Active Orders (after Weekly Softcap): Uncapped
      • 10 per 10 Normal, 20 per Legendary, 30 per Exotic. Legendary Order earn rate has been doubled.
    • Vanguard Alerts: 250 each (up from 50)
      • This can be earned twice a week.
    • Increased total Bright Dust available on the Triumphant Rewards Pass. See the Rewards Pass section below for more details.

Chronologs

General Updates

  • Chronologs will now be one of the four featured currencies shown across the UI, listed after Glimmer and before Silver.
  • Commander Zavala’s offer to acquire the previous week’s Vanguard Arsenal Credits will no longer cost Bright Dust. It will instead cost up to 200 Chronologs:
    • 100 Chronologs awards the first Arsenal Credit, unlocking three weapon choices
    • The fourth and fifth weapon choices can be unlocked for 50 Chronologs each.
  • As before, offers will still only be displayed for Arsenal Credits that were not earned from the Seasonal Hub’s Weekly Reward Track in the previous week.
  • Increased Chronolog gains across all Weekly sources.
    • Weekly Reward Track: 300 total (Up from 50).
    • Vanguard Alerts: 200 total (Up from 50).
      • This can be earned once a week.
    • Additionally, 3500 Chronologs will be available on the Triumphant Rewards Pass.

Vendors

Ada-1

  • Added additional shaders to Ada-1’s shader inventory:
    • Year 1 Shader Pack Shaders
    • Four Unreleased Shaders:
      • Alchemy Scorch
      • Bonemeal Loam
      • Brineglow
      • Charred Plumage
    • Increased number of available shaders per week from three to six.
      • Still randomly rotates through available shaders each week.
    • Updated Ada-1’s Legacy Armor to no longer be on a rotation. Expanded selection to all armors for transmog purposes.
    • Updated Threader Bounty Reset:
      • No longer resets seasonally, instead players will earn one additional threader bounty, per class, per week.
        • New bounties available each week at weekly reset.
        • Maximum number of available Threader Bounties, per class, is ten.

Drifter

  • Gambit Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
  • Updated Gambit Focusing:
    • Gear additions
    • Tired focusing will utilize Gambit engrams.
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Gambit gear.

Lord Shaxx

  • Crucible Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
  • Updated Crucible Focusing:
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Crucible and Competitive Crucible gear.
      • Gear additions
      • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.
      • Tiered Focusing will utilize Crucible engrams.
        • Competitive Crucible gear will require Crucible Ciphers which can be obtained via Competitive Crucible.

Commander Zavala

  • Vanguard Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
  • Updated Vanguard Focusing:
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Solo Ops, Fireteam Ops, Arena Ops and Pinnacle Ops gear.
      • Gear additions
      • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.
      • Tiered Focusing will utilize Vanguard engrams.
        • Pinnacle Ops and Arena Ops gear will require Vanguard Ciphers which can be obtained via Pinnacle Ops and Arena Ops activities.
          • Nightfall Ciphers that players may have in their inventory will be automatically updated to Vanguard Ciphers.
        • Rewards from Call to Arms are available to pick up from Zavala.
        • Updated the rotation for Weekly Vanguard Arms Rewards.

Saint-14

  • Trials of Osiris Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
  • Updated Trials Focusing:
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Trials gear.
      • Gear additions
      • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.
      • Focusing will utilize Trials engrams.

Saladin

  • Updated Iron Banner Focusing:
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Iron Banner gear.
      • Gear additions
      • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.
      • Focusing will utilize Iron Banner Ciphers, obtained through Iron Banner match completions.
    • Iron Banner will maintain Event Card, and will not see it’s Reputation Rank Reward track restored.

Banshee-44

  • Gunsmith Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
    • Dismantling weapons and armors gives Gunsmith Reputation again.
  • Updated Gunsmith Focusing:
    • Now includes Shared Pool weapons from previous releases.
  • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.

Hawthorne

  • Updated Clan engrams to give tiered gear.

Xûr

  • Xûr’s Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
    • Purchasing items from Xur grants reputation.
  • Xûr’s Inventory has been updated:
    • New Catalysts
    • Tiered Armor

Lodi

  • Lodi is now in the Tower. You can find him enjoying his coffee in the Bazaar near Ikora, upstairs in New Monarchy’s old space.

Identity and Expression

Expanded Ornaments for Exotic Armor

  • All qualities of Armor Ornament from Common to Legendary can now be applied to the currently equipped Exotic armor alongside Universal Exotic Ornaments.
    • If the ornament is not specifically parented to the equipped Exotic armor, its visuals will be temporarily disabled in PvP content.
    • Special visual cues or effects applied by ornaments that are not specifically parented to the equipped Exotic armor may not be visible.
  • Exotic Ornaments have been added to Collections.

General Fixes

  • The Death+∞ ornament for Suros Regime will now appear in Collections.
  • The icon for the Reefmade shader has been updated to better communicate its purple themes.
  • The tooltips for the Singular Mercenary, Devoted Warrior, and Noble Partisan armor ornament sets will now correctly note these are associated with a partnership.
  • The Polar Wolf helm for Hunters inadvertently shipped without its ears. We have added a second version of the ornament that includes the ears while leaving the original intact. Existing and future owners of the Hunter version of the Polar Wolf Set can claim this additional version from the Special Deliveries kiosk in the Tower.
  • The Positive Practice emote inadvertently shipped without its intended audio. This has been added to the emote. We’re investigating adding an alternate version of this emote without its audio for those who may prefer that version.
  • Updated the Chlorophyta Mask/Helm/Hood from the Reclamation Rewards Pass to reduce a persistent yellow effect, better aligning its visuals with the Chlorophyta Armor Set.

General

Product Updates

  • The Year of Prophecy Deluxe Edition and the Year of Prophecy Ultimate Edition have been retired.
    • The New Land Beyond Exotic and its catalyst will now be available to new and existing owners of either The Edge of Fate Standard Edition or Renegades Standard Edition. These can be claimed from the Special Deliveries Kiosk in the Tower.
      • New Land Beyond will also be available on the Monument of Lost Lights for owners of either expansion.
    • The Year of Prophecy Ultimate Edition’s themed armor ornament sets will now be available on the Eververse Partner tab for those who do not own the Year of Prophecy Ultimate Edition. Owners of the Year of Prophecy Ultimate Edition can continue to claim their ornaments from the Special Deliveries kiosk if they have not already.
  • The fourth Rewards Pass for the Year of Prophecy – included in the Deluxe and Ultimate Editions – has been renamed from Shadow & Order Rewards Pass to Triumphant Rewards Pass. As the final Rewards Pass in Destiny 2, the Triumphant Rewards Pass has no pre-planned expiration date.
  • All owners of The Edge of Fate: Standard Edition who had purchased prior to the release of Renegades will now retroactively receive both the Reclamation Rewards Pass and the Ash & Iron Rewards Pass if unowned.
    • All purchases of The Edge of Fate: Standard Edition since Renegades launched already provided both passes. This remains unchanged.
  • All owners of Renegades: Standard Edition will now receive both the Lawless Rewards Pass and the Triumphant Rewards Pass as part of their purchase.
    • This change applies retroactively to existing owners of Renegades Standard Edition. Existing owners will automatically receive access to the Triumphant Rewards Pass upon login to Monument of Triumph.
    • For new and future purchasers of Renegades Standard Edition, the Lawless Rewards Pass will be unlocked on the Monument to Seasons Past.
      • If the account played during Lawless and acquired enough progress to earn ranks, some rewards will be ready to claim.
      • Any remaining items can be acquired for Chronologs like any other expired Pass.
    • Destiny 2: The Collection is now available on all platforms. This collection offers all Standard Editions and Legacy Packs for each expansion in one product, along with the Dungeon Keys for The Witch Queen, Lightfall, and The Final Shape and the four Reward Passes from Year of Prophecy.
      • Dungeon Keys remain available in the Eververse individually or in a pack. These will show as Owned if the Keys are acquired from Destiny 2: The Collection.
    • Adjusted the brightness of the Fate Saga backgrounds on the Timeline to improve readability on a wider range of screen types.
    • Access to The Edge of Fate and Renegades campaigns has been moved to the revamped Destinations screen.
    • The Timeline icon has been updated on the revamped Destinations screen.
    • A Timeline node has been added for the Monument of Triumph era.

Destiny Announcements

  • The Destiny Announcements screen has been added to orbit. This screen houses up to thirty-six of the most recent messages shared by the Destiny team.
    • This will allow you to refer to announcements that you may want or need to re-read, may have missed, or had accidentally dismissed for up to thirty days after receipt, after which they will naturally expire and be removed.
  • The system will now support automatic delivery of messages to the Announcement screen, resulting in fewer pop-up messages overall.
  • The Announcements screen also includes a Featured Item carousel including standing messages and information about relevant content.

Rewards Pass Updates

General

  • The expiration timer has been removed.
  • The repeatable 5-Rank and 10-Rank offers in the Eververse have been retired.

Rewards Track

  • The Triumphant Rewards Pass has received additional cosmetic rewards.
  • The total Bright Dust awarded on the Triumphant Rewards Pass has increased from 10,500 to 14,000 and is free to earn for all players.
  • The first rank of the Free Track on the Triumphant Rewards Pass will award an Eververse Engram.
  • The Score Boost for Portal Activities has been replaced with a Minor XP Boost.
    • The Minor XP Boost provides +3% Personal and Fireteam XP for each level unlocked, up to a maximum of five levels and +15%.
    • This Boost honors Score Boost progression from the Lawless Rewards Pass, with your total Score Boost carried over and converted to the Minor XP Boost.
    • Once an account has earned five levels of the Minor XP Boost across both the Lawless Rewards Pass and the Triumphant Rewards Pass, the Minor XP Boost will be capped at 15%.
      • Any remaining Ranks on the Triumphant Reward Pass that would provide a Minor XP Boost will instead award ten Enhancement Cores.

Monument to Seasons Past Updates

General

  • The Ash & Iron Rewards Pass and the Lawless Rewards Pass have been added to the Monument to Seasons Past.
  • The seasonal armor bundles for the Year of The Final Shape have been added to their relevant Episodes.

Exchange

  • The Chronolog Exchange has been updated to support a weekly or bi-weekly purchase cycle for every resource. Below we’ve listed the items offered with their individual Chronolog (CL) cost and how many can be purchased per-week:
    • Enhancement Prism: 50 Chronologs, 10x per week.
    • Ascendant Alloy: 200 Chronologs, 1x per week.
    • Ascendant Shard: 300 Chronologs, 1x per week.
    • Deepsight Harmonizer: 300 Chronologs, 1x per two weeks.
    • 7x Strange Coins: 50 Chronologs, 10x per week.
    • Synthweave Template: 500 Chronologs, 1x per two weeks.
    • Exotic Cipher: 500 Chronologs, 1x per week.
    • Bright Engram: 200 Chronologs, 2x per week.
    • Exotic Engram: 300 Chronologs, 1x per week.

Evergreen Eververse

General Updates

  • Silver and Bright Dust will continue to be relevant currencies in Destiny 2 – any reserves will retain their value.
  • The Recommendations System has been retired.
  • Ornament Sales from the Appearance screen will feature both Silver and Bright Dust offers from The Witch Queen to today.
    • Older items can be acquired from Daily Offers or Bright Engram Focusing in the Eververse.
  • The final slate of new Eververse items will be listed on the Featured Tab through June 23, and on the Seasonal Offerings tab thereafter.
  • The final Foundation event in the Eververse will feature the new Vivid Spectrum Holochip alongside a rerun of last year’s Pride Shaders through June 23.

Daily Offers

  • The Daily Offers tab offers a different selection of Bright Dust and Silver offers each day, drawn from across the history of Destiny 2, including Monument of Triumph.
  • While all players will see the same slate of offers each day, each offer slot is like a deck of cards: any item appearing in a slot will always appear in the same slot again at a future date, but each item in that slot’s deck must appear once before the slot will reshuffle its contents and start again.
  • Daily Offer categories include:
    • For Bright Dust: an Exotic Weapon Ornament slot, an Armor Ornament slot, an Exotic emote slot, a slot for each major Exotic accessory (Ghost Shell, Ship, and Vehicle), two emote slots, a Legendary Ghost Projection Slot, four Legendary Shader slots, and three Legendary Transmat Effect slots
      • The Armor Ornament slot includes both Exotic Armor Ornaments and Legendary Armor Augments that pair with an Exotic armor for the current class, but not individual parts of Legendary Armor Ornament Sets. These can instead be acquired as packs or from Focused Engrams.
      • The Vehicle slot includes both Sparrows and Skimmers.
      • The Emote slots include both Legendary and Rare emotes.
    • For Silver: two Exotic emote slots, one slot for each major Exotic Accessory (Ghost Shell, Ship, and Vehicle), and three slots for Legendary Emotes and Finishers.
      • The Vehicle slot includes both Sparrows and Skimmers.
    • No items are shared between slots of the same currency and type. For example, the Grillmaster Ghost Shell can appear for Silver or Bright Dust, but the Occultation Shader will only appear in one of the four Shader Bright Dust slots.
      • The same item can appear for Silver and Bright Dust in the same day, just like two independent decks of cards can both have the same card next in line at the same time.
    • Daily Offers will host a selection of items – specifically Ghost Shells, Ships, Sparrows, Skimmers, and an emote – that are uniquely available only through Daily Offers or Bright Engram Focusing.
    • After June 23, the Featured Daily Offers tab will replace Featured Items as the Eververse’s landing screen. This tab will feature the Daily Silver Offers, an offer for a single Synthweave Template, and a link to the full Daily Offers tab.

Legends

  • The Legends tab has been refreshed to house content from our Featured Partners.
    • Items on this tab will be available for as long as Destiny 2 is permitted to offer them, after which they will be retired.
    • Partner items will only be available for Silver and will not be available outside of this tab.
    • When all items in the Legends tab are eventually retired, this tab will automatically be removed from the Eververse.

Seasonal Offerings / Archive

  • Seasonal Offerings will permanently house Armor Ornament Set Packs, Weapon Ornament Packs, Shader Packs, Skimmer Packs, and Holochips added with Monument of Triumph, as well as Monument of Triumph’s new Weapon Ornaments, Exotic Armor Ornaments, and Legendary Augments.
  • The Archive will house Armor Ornament Set Packs, Weapon Ornament Packs, Shader Packs, Skimmer Packs, and Holochips offered prior to Monument of Triumph.
    • This includes all previous Holiday Event armors, available for both Silver and Bright Dust.
  • Except for the free Runner Kit, the full contents of each pack in Seasonal Offerings or the Archive – including every Silver-only Armor Ornament Set and new item added in Monument of Triumph – can also be acquired through Focused Bright Engrams.
    • Any components of a pack that match the category of a Daily Offer slot, like a Weapon Ornament, will eventually appear in Daily Offers as well.
  • Filtering Options have been adjusted for the new functionalities.

Bright Engram Focusing

  • Added Bright Engram Focusing to the Eververse.
  • A Focused Engram includes items released through Monument of Triumph, even some items previously unavailable for Bright Dust, and will award an unowned item of the Engram’s category.
  • Several Focused Engrams have more items in them than can be shown in the preview of their contents, but will not drop duplicates, and their contents are finite.
    • When a Focused Engram has been completely emptied, its preview will be empty and additional purchases of that Engram will be blocked.
  • Bright Engram Focusing costs per category are:
For one (1) Eververse Engram +
0 Bright Dust · Standard Decryption with no focusing
300 Bright Dust · Legendary Accessories – Ghosts, Ships, and Vehicles together

· Legendary Shaders

· Legendary Flair – Holochips, Ghost Holograms, and Transmat Effects together.
600 Bright Dust · Exotic Accessories – Ghosts, Ships, Vehicles individually, with Skimmers included in Vehicles.

· Legendary Emotes – Single-Player & Multiplayer together

· Legendary Finishers
900 Bright Dust · Exotic Weapon Ornaments

· Exotic Emotes – Single-Player & Multiplayer together

· Legendary & Exotic Armor Ornaments – individual engrams per class and slot combination.

Legacy Engrams

  • Bright Engram Focusing will also provide a one-to-one exchange for Legacy Engrams if present in your Engram inventory. Eligible legacy engrams include:
S1: Bright Engram S4: Steadfast Engram S6: Jubilant Engram
S2: Illuminated Engram S4: Ephemeral Engram S7: Best of Year 1 Engram
S2: Dawning Engram S5: Etched Engram S8: Nostalgic Engram
S2: Crimson Engram S5: Winterdrift Engram S9: Fond Memories Engram
S3: Prototype Engram S5: Crimson Engram S10: Luminous Engram
S3: Solstice Engram S6: Notorious Engram S11: Mnemonic Engram


https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/destiny-2-server-issues-hundreds-thousands-players-return/

item.title: God of War Laufey Director Pushes Back on Cosmic Cube Criticism: Phranque Was Always In and Enables “Super Cool” Mechanics
item.content:encoded:
God of War Laufey's reveal at the recent June 2026 State of Play certainly pleased many franchise fans, but that's not to say there weren't criticisms to be found online. For example, some wrinkled their noses at Phranque, a talking cosmic jelly cube companion played by actor Jack Quaid, largely known for his role of Hughie in the TV series The Boys (but also already present in God of War: Sons of Sparta as Eis). Phranque, like the sword's talking ribbon, Rue, was criticized for looking whimsical and therefore not grounded enough for some tastes.

The topic was tangentially touched upon by Game Director Ariel Lawrence in a recent interview with YouTuber FlowGames (spotted by ResetEra's Angie). Lawrence, who worked on the original God of War games before leaving for a stint at Riot Games, returned in July 2021 and found that Phranque (alongside Faye, of course) was the first character established by Head of Creative Cory Barlog when laying the foundation for God of War Laufey. She also said that Phranque's cubic nature has enabled the team to implement "super cool" mechanics for combat, traversal, and puzzle-solving.

Both Phranque and Rue are really important, both in combat assists as supplemental to Fay, but also in traversal and puzzle solving, and just the utility of both of them and what they can do in the shapes that they're in. So, for Phranque, as a cosmic jelly cube, how we may use him in certain parts of the world has definitely been exciting for the team just in terms of design work for us.

When I came back to the team, the two characters that actually were started from with Corey [Barlog] were both Faye and Phranque. Phranque was one of the very first things he's always been a part of this story in the Everywhen. And I think just that possibility of taking something that feels kind of inanimate and not a person, and really seeing how we could drive connection and story and make you feel about a cube. I think that was just an interesting challenge and pitch, and Cory and I were talking about it. I was like, "That's really exciting. That's something different." And I think that at the heart of it, too, it's just the surprise factor of it that was just so exciting for the team. And where we could go with that mechanically has also been super cool.

Indeed, I can already picture plenty of interesting applications. It would be even better if we could direct the cube ourselves, though that might not be the case. Anyway, talking objects aren't exactly news to the fantasy genre; as always, to judge a character's depth and value, we'll have to play the game ourselves. God of War Laufey doesn't have an official release window yet, but it is expected to launch next year on PlayStation 5, blending the brutal combat of the Norse era with the fluidity of the Greek era.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/god-of-war-laufey-phranque-cosmic-cube/

item.title: You'll be able to change some parts of Final Fantasy 7 Revelation's story, but no matter what there's only one ending
item.content:encoded:
To mosey, or not to mosey

Last week, Square Enix graced us with the revelation that the final instalment of the Final Fantasy 7 Remake trilogy is just that, Final Fantasy 7 Revelation. It'll obviously follow on from Rebirth, which ends with [REDACTED], but where it goes from there is still generally a mystery given the various changes made so far. As it turns out, you'll be able to make a few changes yourself, except for one, important thing: the ending.

Speaking to GamesRadar, director Naoki Hamaguchi explained that Revelation will have a number of "player choices" that affect certain story beats. "As our party head into the final battle, the game is going to show the resolve that they carry within them," he explains. "This goes without saying, but our heroes, Cloud and his companions, are beloved by a lot of fans around the world. But when we thought about that, we didn't want to just kind of deliver a singular storytelling experience for every single player around the world. We wanted each player – each fan – to have a unique experience as they head on to the final battle."

When it comes to that final battle, Hamaguchi notes that it's "probably going to receive a lot of attention from our fans," and that he thinks "a lot of fans are worrying – or just being curious – about how it's going to play out in the end." However it might end up, he does say that the "ending of the story is going to be a singular ending," meaning that your choices won't change the story's fate ultimately. But, you might still go into the ending with different feelings compared to a friend who chose differently.

"There won't be multiple endings that would change depending on the players' choices, but the process of how we get there to that singular ending might be a bit different between the players based on the choices that they make throughout the game. That might mean that some players might have a different emotion as they get to the story ending, so that's kind of the difference that they might have."

Revelation, and the remake trilogy as a whole, will likely be a smidge polarising no matter how it ends up, and would be so whether you had any choice or not. This is Final Fantasy 7 after all, a game that has almost morphed into a biblical text for many. We'll just have to wait and see if it remotely pulls anything off!



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/youll-be-able-to-change-some-parts-of-final-fantasy-7-revelations-story-but-no-matter-what-theres-only-one-ending

item.title: The Physical Version Of Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave Is An $80 Game On Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
We’re in the age of what Nintendo is calling “variable pricing.” Some games are $60. Some are less. And a few are a decent bit more. Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave is one of those. The physical version of the strategy RPG will run $80, matching the price of Mario Kart World and the Switch 2 edition of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom, Nintendo’s only other $80 games.

Out September 17, 2026, Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave is now viewable on the eShop along with its unexpected price. While the digital price runs $70, the standard amount for new premium boxed games on Switch 2, the physical version is $10 more. That’ll be a tough pill to swallow for fans who prefer to have their Fire Emblem games and SRPGs sitting on their shelves next to them.

Huh, Fire Emblem is 80 dollars physically pic.twitter.com/Is8GXxgX5q

— Spawn Wave (@SpawnWaveMedia) June 9, 2026

Earlier this year, Nintendo announced that it would begin making the digital versions of its games $10 cheaper than the physical versions. The idea was that it would be a savings to people who download the games compared to those who get them the old-fashioned way. But it’s hard to imagine $80 being the “natural” price for Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave rather than an amount that includes extra to cover the cost of storage on SD Express cards in the current AI-fueled RAM apocalypse.

Every other game Nintendo has announced or launched since the new digital discount was announced has been $70 or less. Star Fox is $60 physical and $50 for a digital copy. Yoshi and the Mysterious Book is $70 for physical and $60 for digital. The lack of any consistent pattern has made it hard to pin down whether the digital discount is really a price hike on physical in disguise, but it certainly feels that way with Fire Emblem.



https://kotaku.com/the-physical-version-of-fire-emblem-fortunes-weave-is-an-80-game-on-switch-2-2000704496

item.title: Destiny 2 Fans Break Servers & Marathon’s Peak Player Count In Final Update
item.content:encoded:
The dreaded day has finally arrived, as Destiny 2 has received its final ever update, with the Monument of Triumph. Fans have turned up in droves to bid farewell to the game, and in doing so, they’ve already smashed Marathon’s peak player count, with the number still rapidly rising.

The influx of players has been so significant that many fans trying to log in were met with errors, and those attempting to track the milestone found SteamDB struggling to keep up, with the site seemingly going down altogether!

Battle Of The Player Counts

According to SteamDB, Marathon’s peak player count was just over 88,000, but at the time of writing, just 20 odd minutes after the update, Destiny 2’s is over 100,000, and still rapidly climbing—at least, until it crashed.

It’s worth once again reiterating that a high percentage of Marathon’s player base is also on Steam, whilst the opposite can be said for Destiny 2, which has a high console player base. You can expect the total numbers to be far higher than just what SteamDB has to show.

With Marathon Season 2 just kicking off, as well as coming off the back of a free week, Destiny 2’s player count tells the story that there’s a much higher demand for Destiny, as more questions pour in over Bungie’s decision to kill the game—and shift its focus to Marathon instead.

The goal was always to slam the servers of Destiny 2 with its final update, as fans aim to send a message to both Sony and Bungie that they aren’t done with the Destiny franchise. In fact, a petition was even made, which reached over 350,000 signatures, calling for the development of Destiny 3, since the sequel is seemingly nowhere in sight.

With the player count of Destiny 2 today, as well as the general call for Destiny 3 to be made, it’s blatant that fans aren’t ready to say goodbye. Unfortunately, that decision isn’t up to the fans, or even the hundreds of developers at Bungie, and until a new project is greenlit, all fans can do is enjoy the final state of Destiny 2.

That said, saying goodbye hasn’t been easy. In fact, as if it couldn’t get any more difficult, the new home screen for Destiny is literally a sun setting under the title card—it couldn’t be any more on the nose.

Will you be jumping into Destiny 2 during the Monument of Triumph update? And is this a final goodbye for the franchise? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about everything that was revealed during the Nintendo Direct, as well as the potential release date for Persona 6.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
